libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: Configure generated tests to pass these options both to JVM test execution and to GraalVM native-image build arguments: --add-opens=java.base/java.lang=ALL-UNNAMED, --add-opens=java.base/java.net=ALL-UNNAMED, and --add-opens=java.base/sun.nio.ch=ALL-UNNAMED. Tests should call Conscrypt.checkAvailability(), create a provider with Conscrypt.newProvider(), and prefer loopback/local TLS or JCA operations rather than external network services.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8879 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.conscrypt:conscrypt-openjdk-uber:1.4.1; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: The artifact bundles platform native libraries under META-INF/native and extracts/loads them at runtime; failures in native-image are more likely reachability/JNI/resource metadata issues than missing dependencies.

If the module opens are omitted, coverage that touches SSLSocket/SocketChannel or native library helper loading can fail on strongly encapsulated JDKs before it reaches the intended Conscrypt behavior.
No Docker image appears necessary unless the generated tests deliberately choose to validate against an external TLS service, which is not required for meaningful coverage.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: Conscrypt's OpenJDK uber artifact is self-contained and has no Maven runtime dependencies, and normal meaningful tests can use local JSSE/JCA APIs without Docker. However, Conscrypt 1.4.1 reflectively accesses JDK internals for native-library loading and socket/file-descriptor handling, so generated tests that exercise real Conscrypt sockets/provider behavior need explicit module opens on modern JDKs and in native-image builds.
